seoTitle: How to Read Data From an API in Power BI
metaDescription: Connect Power BI to a REST API using the Web data source, pass secure token headers, and shape raw JSON into usable tables with Power Query.
intro: Manually copying raw JSON strings from an API response is slow and breaks the moment a server schema shifts. Power BI's Web data source combined with Power Query's transformation tools lets you build a direct, repeatable connection to external APIs. This guide covers setting up the connection and shaping the returned data.

steps.heading: Format the endpoint URL
steps.body: Set up the request URL, including the base route and any relative paths, inside Power BI's connector window.

steps.heading: Add authorization headers
steps.body: Insert custom authorization tokens or API keys as request headers so secured endpoints accept the connection.

steps.heading: Convert JSON into a table structure
steps.body: Use Power Query to convert the raw JSON elements returned by the API into a proper tabular list structure.

steps.heading: Expand layered records
steps.body: Expand any nested records or lists within the JSON response into individual columns you can use in your data model.

steps.heading: Publish and schedule refresh
steps.body: Publish the report to the cloud service and configure a scheduled refresh so the API data stays current automatically.

faq.question: Do I need coding skills to connect Power BI to an API?
faq.answer: Basic familiarity with URLs and headers helps, but Power Query's interface handles most of the JSON transformation without requiring code.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with a secured API?
faq.answer: You typically add a token or API key as a custom header in the Web connector's advanced options.

faq.question: Why is my JSON data showing as a single column of records?
faq.answer: This is normal at first; you need to expand the nested record or list column in Power Query to break it into individual usable columns.

faq.question: Can scheduled refresh work with an authenticated API?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as your credentials are properly saved in the data source settings, scheduled refresh can continue authenticating automatically.
